body.title: Identify what keeps re-firing blocked HIGH-inertia writes — habit or goal stuck in re-attempt loop
body.result: Root cause: goal_continuation step 4 re-emitted GOAL_READY even when ticket was awaiting_approval/blocked, causing PROC_CODING_SPRINT → pe_chain → SCOPE_GUARD escalate loop every 2 min. Fix: check _load_ticket status before re-emitting; skip if blocked or awaiting_approval. 2 new regression tests.
body.description:
T-scope-guard-echo-dedup (d121605f) silenced the echo. This ticket attacks the underlying re-attempt.

## Observation
Three operations keep firing at 10-60 min intervals even though they're always blocked:
- write wild_igor/igor/cognition/thalamus.py (MEDIUM inertia, warning)
- write wild_igor/igor/brainstem/kernel.py (HIGH inertia, blocked)
- delete wild_igor/igor/memory/models.py (HIGH inertia, blocked)

Each attempt goes through SCOPE_GUARD → escalate → pe_chain close-goal. The goal close SHOULD prevent re-firing (see pe_chain.py:1777-1786 close_goal_by_ticket), but something is re-creating an equivalent goal each cycle.

## Suspect paths to investigate
1. Is there a habit (PROC_*) that's hardcoded to produce these particular edit intentions? Check habit chunker + seed-habit data for anything mentioning brainstem/kernel or memory/models.
2. Is close_goal_by_ticket actually succeeding? The pe_chain line 1781 passes the ticket_id; check whether the close is logging an error silently, and whether the goal table still shows matching active goals post-close.
3. Is an LLM-generated 'next step' producing these intents? A self-improvement habit that keeps choosing the same files would do this. Look at recent traces (mcp__igor__traces_recent) around the block-message timestamps.
4. Is the ticket_id stable across re-attempts? If each attempt gets a fresh UUID-style ticket_id, close_goal can't match and the suppression doesn't stick. If it IS stable (likely, from log output 'unknown'), see #2.

## Starter query
After Igor restart, run:
  SELECT * FROM clan.memories WHERE memory_type = 'EPISODIC' AND metadata::text ILIKE '%brainstem%' AND timestamp > (now() - interval '24 hours')::text ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 10;

Should show the goal/habit nodes firing these attempts. The narrative + metadata together should identify the source.

## Success criteria
- No more re-attempts of the same (file, op) after a reasonable window
- Forensic log shows WHY it was firing (habit id, goal lineage)
- If it's a legitimate habit with a bad target — fix the target. If it's a runaway — close the underlying attractor.

## Provenance
Akien + CC 2026-04-18b, during /readigor diagnosis. Parent: T-scope-guard-echo-dedup (symptom fix).
